message.content:
[CHAT-M1] Build self-hosted chat system from scratch as standalone project. Goal: fully replace Stream Chat in Datetris with own infrastructure.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Real-time messaging (WebSocket-based)
- 1-on-1 chat between matched users
- Private gallery: upload photos/videos to your own backend
- Send media in chat as expiring messages (single-view OR time-based expiry)
- Server-side enforcement of expiry (delete file from disk after view/timer)
- Push notification hooks (later integration with FCM/APNs)
- Read receipts, typing indicators
- Message history sync across devices
- E2E encryption (nice-to-have, not required v1)

TECH STACK PREFERENCE:
- Backend: Python FastAPI + WebSocket (matches Datetris stack)
- Frontend: Flutter widgets that drop into FrontA20 chat_screen.dart later
- Storage: SQLite for messages (matches Datetris), separate folder for media
- Authentication: reuse JWT from Datetris main backend (not duplicate auth)

DELIVERABLES:
1. Full backend repo at /Users/macminiai/projects/ChatA20/backend/
2. Frontend chat module at /Users/macminiai/projects/ChatA20/frontend/
3. README with integration instructions for Datetris
4. Test harness — local two-instance simulation (sender + receiver)
5. Migration guide from Stream Chat to own chat
6. Privacy + security audit notes

PHASE 1 PLAN (don't code yet, plan first):
- Database schema (chats, messages, attachments, expiry_jobs)
- API endpoints list
- WebSocket protocol design
- Media upload + expiry flow
- Integration touchpoints with Datetris main backend (auth check, user lookup, match verification)
- Effort estimate

Reply with PHASE 1 plan + questions before any code is written.

AUTO-APPROVE.
Reply [CHAT-M1].

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
[CHAT-M1]

# PHASE 1 PLAN — Self-Hosted Chat System

## 1. Database Schema (SQLite)

```sql
-- Conversations between two matched users
chats (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,           -- UUID
  user_a_id TEXT NOT NULL,       -- Datetris user IDs (sorted lexicographically)
  user_b_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  match_id TEXT,                 -- reference to Datetris match
  created_at INTEGER,
  last_message_at INTEGER,
  UNIQUE(user_a_id, user_b_id)
)
CREATE INDEX idx_chats_user_a ON chats(user_a_id, last_message_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_chats_user_b ON chats(user_b_id, last_message_at DESC);

-- Messages
messages (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,           -- UUID (client-generated for idempotency)
  chat_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES chats(id),
  sender_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  type TEXT NOT NULL,            -- 'text' | 'image' | 'video' | 'system'
  body TEXT,                     -- text content (null for media)
  attachment_id TEXT REFERENCES attachments(id),
  reply_to_id TEXT,
  client_ts INTEGER,             -- client-side timestamp
  server_ts INTEGER NOT NULL,    -- authoritative
  edited_at INTEGER,
  deleted_at INTEGER,            -- tombstone
  read_by_recipient_at INTEGER,
  delivered_at INTEGER
)
CREATE INDEX idx_messages_chat_ts ON messages(chat_id, server_ts DESC);

-- Media attachments with expiry
attachments (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  owner_id TEXT NOT NULL,        -- uploader
  chat_id TEXT,                  -- null until attached to message
  file_path TEXT NOT NULL,       -- absolute path on disk
  mime_type TEXT,
  size_bytes INTEGER,
  width INTEGER,
  height INTEGER,
  duration_ms INTEGER,           -- for video
  thumbnail_path TEXT,
  expiry_mode TEXT,              -- 'single_view' | 'timer' | 'none'
  expiry_seconds INTEGER,        -- for timer mode, starts on first view
  first_viewed_at INTEGER,
  viewed_by_recipient INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  deleted_at INTEGER,
  created_at INTEGER
)
CREATE INDEX idx_attachments_owner ON attachments(owner_id);

-- Gallery (private per-user media pool before attaching to chat)
gallery_items (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  attachment_id TEXT REFERENCES attachments(id),
  label TEXT,                    -- optional user tag
  created_at INTEGER
)
CREATE INDEX idx_gallery_user ON gallery_items(user_id, created_at DESC);

-- Scheduled deletion jobs (durable, survive restarts)
expiry_jobs (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  attachment_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES attachments(id),
  fire_at INTEGER NOT NULL,      -- unix ts
  processed_at INTEGER
)
CREATE INDEX idx_expiry_fire ON expiry_jobs(fire_at) WHERE processed_at IS NULL;

-- Device registrations for push
devices (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  push_token TEXT,
  platform TEXT,                 -- 'ios' | 'android'
  last_seen_at INTEGER
)

-- Typing / presence is in-memory only (Redis-optional, v1 = process memory)
```

## 2. REST API Endpoints

```
# Chats
GET    /v1/chats                           # list my chats, paginated
POST   /v1/chats                           # create/get chat with {peer_user_id} (validates match via Datetris)
GET    /v1/chats/{chat_id}/messages        # history, cursor-based (?before=server_ts&limit=50)
POST   /v1/chats/{chat_id}/messages        # send (fallback for offline-send; normally via WS)
POST   /v1/chats/{chat_id}/read            # mark read up to {message_id}

# Gallery
GET    /v1/gallery                         # my private media
POST   /v1/gallery/upload                  # multipart upload → returns attachment_id
DELETE /v1/gallery/{item_id}

# Attachments
POST   /v1/attachments                     # init upload; returns {attachment_id, upload_url}
PUT    /v1/attachments/{id}/content        # stream bytes (chunked)
POST   /v1/attachments/{id}/finalize       # commit + generate thumbnail
GET    /v1/attachments/{id}/content        # authed read, enforces expiry, triggers single-view consume
GET    /v1/attachments/{id}/thumbnail      # lighter, non-expiring preview (optional)

# Devices / push
POST   /v1/devices                         # register FCM/APNs token
DELETE /v1/devices/{id}

# Health
GET    /healthz
GET    /readyz
```

All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <datetris_jwt>` — the chat backend validates the JWT signature using Datetris's public key (or shared secret), extracts `user_id`, does not issue its own tokens.

## 3. WebSocket Protocol

**Endpoint:** `wss://chat.datetris.example/v1/ws?token=<jwt>`

**Framing:** JSON envelopes, one message per frame.

```jsonc
// Client → Server
{ "type": "send", "client_msg_id": "uuid", "chat_id": "...", "msg_type": "text", "body": "hi" }
{ "type": "send", "client_msg_id": "uuid", "chat_id": "...", "msg_type": "image", "attachment_id": "..." }
{ "type": "typing", "chat_id": "...", "state": "start" | "stop" }
{ "type": "read", "chat_id": "...", "up_to_message_id": "..." }
{ "type": "view_attachment", "attachment_id": "..." }   // triggers single-view consumption
{ "type": "ack", "server_msg_id": "..." }               // delivery ack
{ "type": "ping" }

// Server → Client
{ "type": "message", "message": { ...full message record... } }
{ "type": "message_ack", "client_msg_id": "...", "server_msg_id": "...", "server_ts": 123 }
{ "type": "typing", "chat_id": "...", "user_id": "...", "state": "start" }
{ "type": "read", "chat_id": "...", "user_id": "...", "up_to_message_id": "..." }
{ "type": "attachment_expired", "attachment_id": "..." }
{ "type": "presence", "user_id": "...", "online": true }
{ "type": "error", "code": "...", "message": "..." }
{ "type": "pong" }
```

**Key behaviors:**
- Client sends `client_msg_id` for idempotency; server returns `message_ack` with `server_msg_id` + `server_ts`.
- Offline delivery: on reconnect, client sends last `server_ts` seen per chat → server replays missed messages.
- Typing indicators: ephemeral, not persisted, 5s auto-expire if no `stop`.
- Heartbeat: client ping every 20s; server closes idle connections after 60s.

## 4. Media Upload + Expiry Flow

**Upload (two-phase, resumable-friendly):**
1. `POST /v1/attachments` with metadata → returns `attachment_id` and `upload_url`.
2. `PUT /v1/attachments/{id}/content` streams bytes (supports chunked with `Content-Range`).
3. `POST /v1/attachments/{id}/finalize` → server generates thumbnail (ffmpeg for video, Pillow for images), commits row.
4. Client sends a `type=image|video` WS message referencing `attachment_id` + `expiry_mode` + `expiry_seconds`.

**Storage layout:**
```
/var/chat-media/
  <user_id>/<yyyy-mm>/<attachment_id>.<ext>
  <user_id>/<yyyy-mm>/<attachment_id>.thumb.jpg
```
Files are never served by a static web server — always through authed endpoint that checks: caller is sender or recipient of a message that references this attachment, and not expired.

**Expiry enforcement (server-authoritative):**

*Single-view:*
- On first GET `/content` by recipient: set `first_viewed_at`, `viewed_by_recipient=1`, enqueue `expiry_jobs` row with `fire_at = now + 5s` (small grace for media player buffering). Background worker deletes file + marks `attachments.deleted_at`.
- Subsequent GETs return 410 Gone.

*Timer (e.g., 10s after first open):*
- On first view: set `first_viewed_at`, enqueue job at `first_viewed_at + expiry_seconds`.
- Client also shows countdown, but server does not rely on client to delete.

*Background worker:*
- Async loop polls `expiry_jobs WHERE fire_at <= now AND processed_at IS NULL LIMIT 100` every 2s.
- Deletes file from disk, zeros out `file_path`, sets `deleted_at`, broadcasts `attachment_expired` WS event to both participants.
- Durable: survives restarts because jobs are in SQLite.

*Screenshot/screen-record:* cannot be prevented server-side. Client-side mitigations (iOS flag, Android FLAG_SECURE) are frontend concerns; backend can log view events so sender can see recipient viewed.

## 5. Datetris Integration Touchpoints

The chat backend treats Datetris main backend as source of truth for identity and match state. Chat backend has **no user table**, **no auth logic of its own**.

| Touchpoint | Direction | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| JWT verification | Chat → Datetris | Shared signing key OR `GET /auth/jwks.json` from Datetris; verify locally, no per-request call |
| Match verification | Chat → Datetris | `GET /internal/matches/check?user_a=&user_b=` (service-to-service auth via internal API key) — called only when creating a new chat, result cached 60s |
| User profile lookup | Chat → Datetris | `GET /internal/users/{id}/public` for display name/avatar — cached 5min |
| Unmatch event | Datetris → Chat | Webhook `POST /internal/events/unmatched {user_a, user_b}` → chat marks conversation archived, blocks new messages |
| User deletion | Datetris → Chat | Webhook `POST /internal/events/user_deleted {user_id}` → cascade delete messages + attachments |
| Push notifications | Chat → Datetris OR direct | Option A: chat calls Datetris `POST /internal/notifications/push`; Option B: chat holds FCM/APNs creds directly. **Recommend A** — keeps push infra centralized |

## 6. Repo Layout

```
ChatA20/
  backend/
    app/
      main.py                    # FastAPI app + WS mount
      auth.py                    # JWT verify, Datetris client
      db.py                      # SQLite connection + migrations
      models.py                  # Pydantic + row adapters
      routers/
        chats.py messages.py gallery.py attachments.py devices.py
      ws/
        connection.py            # per-user socket registry
        handlers.py              # envelope dispatcher
        presence.py
      services/
        expiry_worker.py
        media.py                 # thumbnail gen, mime sniff
        datetris_client.py
      migrations/
    tests/
      test_http.py test_ws.py test_expiry.py test_integration.py
    pyproject.toml
    README.md
  frontend/
    lib/
      chat/
        chat_client.dart         # WS + REST wrapper
        models/
        widgets/
          message_list.dart message_bubble.dart composer.dart
          expiring_media_viewer.dart typing_indicator.dart
        gallery/
    pubspec.yaml
  docs/
    MIGRATION_FROM_STREAM.md
    SECURITY_AUDIT.md
    INTEGRATION_DATETRIS.md
  harness/
    run_local.sh                 # spins backend + two Flutter instances
    seed.py
```

## 7. Effort Estimate (solo dev, focused)

| Milestone | Scope | Est. |
|---|---|---|
| M2 Backend skeleton | FastAPI app, JWT verify, DB migrations, chat + message CRUD | 2–3 d |
| M3 WebSocket layer | Connection mgr, envelope protocol, send/read/typing, reconnect replay | 3–4 d |
| M4 Media pipeline | Upload, thumbnail, authed serving, gallery | 2–3 d |
| M5 Expiry engine | Single-view + timer, durable worker, broadcast events | 2 d |
| M6 Datetris integration | JWT verify against real keys, match check, webhooks, push hook | 2 d |
| M7 Flutter chat module | Client, widgets, expiring viewer, parity with Stream UI | 4–6 d |
| M8 Test harness | Two-instance local sim, integration tests | 2 d |
| M9 Migration guide + security audit + README | docs | 1–2 d |
| **Total** | | **~18–24 dev-days** |

## Open Questions Before Coding

1. **Deployment target:** Single VM (systemd + nginx TLS) or containerized (Docker Compose) alongside Datetris? Affects media path + TLS handling.
2. **Scale assumption for v1:** how many concurrent users do we need to support? Under ~5k concurrent, single-process FastAPI + SQLite WAL is fine. Beyond that we need Postgres + Redis pub/sub for WS fanout across workers. Please confirm v1 target.
3. **Datetris JWT format:** HS256 with shared secret, or RS256 with JWKS? Need the claim name for user id (`sub`? `user_id`?) and any existing token TTL.
4. **Datetris "internal" API:** does it already expose service-to-service endpoints, or do I need to add `/internal/matches/check` etc. on the Datetris side as part of this work?
5. **Match model:** can a pair chat *before* matching (e.g., intro message), or strictly post-match only? Affects the match-verification gate.
6. **Unmatch semantics:** on unmatch — archive & hide (recoverable) or hard-delete all history + media? Dating-app norms vary.
7. **Expiry defaults:** what are the UX-level options shown in FrontA20? (e.g., "view once", "10s timer", "no expiry"). Need exact list to wire up.
8. **Media size caps:** max photo MB, max video MB, max video duration? Drives upload validation and thumbnail strategy.
9. **Push:** route via Datetris (recommended) or stand up FCM/APNs directly in chat service? If routed, Datetris needs a `POST /internal/notifications/push` endpoint.
10. **Read receipts visibility:** always on, or user-toggleable? Affects schema flag on the user side (which lives in Datetris).
11. **E2E encryption:** confirmed deferred past v1? If so I'll design the message schema so `body` could later become ciphertext with a key-exchange layer added, without breaking migration.
12. **Backup/retention:** how long do non-expiring messages live? Forever, or auto-purged after N days of conversation inactivity?
13. **Profanity / abuse reporting:** any moderation hooks needed in v1, or strictly a later phase?

Once you answer these (or say "use your best judgment on all"), I'll scaffold the backend repo and start on M2.

message.content:
[CHAT-M2-final] Build chat system with PAID-SETUP ARCHITECTURE but ZERO-COST IMPLEMENTATION.

Build it as if every component is a managed service — but use self-hosted free equivalents internally. When upgrading later, only the connection string / endpoint URL changes.

ARCHITECTURE (mimics future paid setup):

1. PostgreSQL (self-hosted on same VM via docker-compose initially, but configured EXACTLY like managed)
   - Connection via DATABASE_URL env var
   - Use postgres-compatible SQL only — no fallback to sqlite
   - When upgrading: just change DATABASE_URL to point at managed instance

2. Redis (self-hosted on same VM via docker-compose)
   - Connection via REDIS_URL env var
   - Used for: WS pub/sub fanout, user profile cache, chat list cache
   - When upgrading: just change REDIS_URL

3. Media storage abstracted via MediaStore interface
   - v1 implementation: LocalDiskMediaStore (writes to /var/chat-media/, serves via authed FastAPI endpoint)
   - Future implementation: S3MediaStore (writes to S3 bucket, returns CloudFront signed URLs)
   - Single env var MEDIA_BACKEND={local|s3} swaps implementation
   - All callers use the abstract interface

4. Cloudflare free tier (TLS + DDoS + WS edge termination + DNS)
   - Configured from day 1
   - WS upgrade headers properly forwarded

5. Cert pinning ready in Flutter client (env var to disable in dev)

6. JWT RS256 with JWKS rotation (NOT HS256 shared secret)
   - Generate RSA keypair locally
   - Publish public key at /v1/auth/jwks.json
   - Datetris will need matching pubkey config later

7. E2E encryption from day 1 (Signal protocol via libsignal-protocol-typescript or equivalent)
   - Server stores ciphertext only
   - Key exchange handled at chat creation
   - Future-proof for end-to-end audit

8. PostgreSQL pgcrypto for at-rest encryption of sensitive cols (message body fallback if E2E disabled)

9. Background worker for expiry/push/thumbnail
   - Standalone process (not in main FastAPI worker)
   - Reads from Redis queue OR Postgres SKIP LOCKED
   - Future: deploy as separate VM or Lambda

10. Containerized via Docker Compose:
    - chat-backend service
    - chat-worker service
    - postgres service
    - redis service
    - All ports exposed via docker-compose.yml
    - Single command to spin up: docker-compose up

11. Health endpoints: /healthz (liveness), /readyz (readiness with DB + Redis check)

12. Structured JSON logging from day 1 (parseable by Datadog/CloudWatch later)

13. Prometheus metrics endpoint /metrics (free local scraping; can wire to managed Prometheus later)

14. nginx in front with HTTP/2, permessage-deflate WS compression, gzip on REST responses

15. Backup strategy:
    - Postgres: nightly pg_dump cron (local now, S3 later)
    - Media: nightly rsync of /var/chat-media to backup directory
    - Redis: ephemeral, no backup needed

16. Stateless backend — no in-process state. All session state in Redis or Postgres.

DOCUMENT in docs/MIGRATION_PATHS.md:
- How to swap LocalDiskMediaStore → S3MediaStore (single env var)
- How to swap self-hosted Postgres → managed Postgres (DATABASE_URL change)
- How to swap self-hosted Redis → managed Redis (REDIS_URL change)
- How to add CDN (env var CDN_URL_PREFIX, MediaStore returns signed URLs to CDN)
- How to add 2nd backend VM (HAProxy or DO LB config example)
- How to multi-region (Postgres read replicas + region routing)

Each migration path = under 1 day of work because architecture already supports it.

PHASE NEXT (don't code yet, plan it):
- Confirm all 13 question answers (already provided in CHAT-M2 message)
- Audit: is anything in PostgreSQL setup that won't translate to managed?
- Audit: is anything in Redis setup that won't translate to managed?
- Audit: is anything in MediaStore abstraction that constrains S3 migration?
- Confirm Docker Compose works on Mac Mini for dev + on prod Droplet

Reply [CHAT-M2-final] with revised plan + audit results.

AUTO-APPROVE.

content.text:
[CHAT-M2-final]

## Context gap to flag first

You referenced "13 question answers already provided in CHAT-M2 message" but I only see your CHAT-M1 reply → this CHAT-M2-final message. The 13 questions from my prior reply are unanswered in this thread. I'll proceed with defaults below (and mark them), but please either paste the answers or confirm the defaults before I scaffold.

**Assumed defaults** (flag any to change):
1. Deployment: single Droplet, Docker Compose, Cloudflare in front. 2. Scale v1: ≤5k concurrent. 3. JWT: RS256/JWKS (per spec). 4. Datetris internal API: I'll add the needed endpoints spec to docs; you wire them in Datetris later. 5. Chat only post-match. 6. Unmatch: archive + hide (recoverable 30d, then purge). 7. Expiry options: view-once, 10s/30s/1min timer, no-expiry. 8. Media caps: 20MB photo, 100MB / 60s video. 9. Push: route via Datetris. 10. Read receipts: user-toggleable (flag lives in Datetris). 11. E2E: **see audit below — recommend deferring Signal to v2, keep schema E2E-ready**. 12. Retention: no auto-purge for non-expiring messages in v1. 13. Moderation: report endpoint only, no active scanning v1.

---

## Audit results

### A. PostgreSQL — self-hosted → managed translation

**Safe (works identically on RDS / Aurora / DO Managed / Neon / Supabase):**
- Standard SQL, indexes, constraints, triggers, views
- `pgcrypto`, `uuid-ossp`, `pg_trgm`, `citext` — available on all major managed providers
- Connection pooling via PgBouncer (some managed include it, otherwise run pgbouncer as its own compose service so the app only ever talks to a pooler — portable)

**Things that will NOT translate — avoiding from day 1:**
- `LISTEN`/`NOTIFY` for app-level eventing → **using Redis pub/sub instead** (PgBouncer transaction mode breaks LISTEN)
- `COPY FROM '/path'` (server-side file) → use `COPY FROM STDIN` only
- Superuser-only ops (`CREATE EXTENSION` outside the allowlist, `pg_read_server_files`, custom C extensions) — we only use pgcrypto/uuid-ossp which are universally allowed
- `postgresql.conf` edits at runtime → all tuning via env/parameter group equivalents; document settings in `docs/POSTGRES_TUNING.md`
- Logical replication slots / `pg_basebackup` → use `pg_dump` for backup, provider snapshots for prod
- Filesystem-level `tablespace` placement → default tablespace only
- `pg_cron` → we run cron outside the DB (host cron + background worker)

**Verdict:** no blockers. One `DATABASE_URL` change + optional PgBouncer endpoint swap.

### B. Redis — self-hosted → managed translation

**Safe (works on ElastiCache / Upstash / DO / Redis Cloud):**
- `PUBLISH`/`SUBSCRIBE`, `SET`/`GET`/`EX`, `INCR`, `HSET`, `ZADD`/`ZRANGE`, `SCAN`, `EXPIRE`
- Lua `EVAL` (small scripts only)
- Redis Streams (`XADD`/`XREADGROUP`) — used for the worker queue

**Things that will NOT translate — avoiding from day 1:**
- `KEYS *` → use `SCAN` with cursor exclusively (cheap in dev too)
- `CONFIG SET`, `FLUSHALL`, `DEBUG`, `SAVE` → never called from app code; only dev tooling
- `MONITOR` → not used
- RedisJSON / RediSearch modules → not used (not on all managed tiers)
- Cross-key transactions / `MULTI`/`EXEC` across unrelated keys → we only use single-key atomic ops; if we ever need multi-key, use hash tags (`{user:123}:typing`, `{user:123}:presence`) so cluster mode works
- Assume single logical DB (DB 0 only) — some managed (ElastiCache cluster) don't support `SELECT`
- Assume no persistence guarantees → all Redis data is regeneratable (cache, ephemeral pub/sub, short-TTL queue entries)

**Verdict:** no blockers. One `REDIS_URL` change.

### C. MediaStore abstraction — constraints for S3 migration

**Interface design** (locked in before coding so S3 swap is trivial):

```python
class MediaStore(Protocol):
    async def init_upload(owner_id, mime, size_hint) -> UploadHandle
    async def write_chunk(handle, offset, data) -> None
    async def finalize(handle) -> StoredObject          # returns opaque object_key
    async def get_serving_url(object_key, viewer_id, ttl_seconds) -> ServingResult
    async def delete(object_key) -> None
    async def stat(object_key) -> ObjectMeta | None
```

`ServingResult` is `{kind: "stream", handler_path}` for local or `{kind: "redirect", signed_url}` for S3. Client always calls our API first; the API decides. **Clients never see raw storage URLs.**

**Constraints identified:**
- **Single-view expiry + signed URLs are fundamentally at odds.** A signed S3 URL cannot be revoked before its TTL. Two mitigations baked into the interface from day 1:
  1. `get_serving_url` accepts a `ttl_seconds` (we use ≤10s for view-once media),
  2. For `expiry_mode == 'single_view'` the app sets `kind='stream'` on both local *and* S3 (proxy bytes through backend), so the server controls when the stream ends and can refuse subsequent requests. S3 bandwidth cost for this is modest because view-once media is viewed once by definition.
- **Chunked upload semantics differ.** Local appends to file; S3 uses multipart upload (min 5MB part except last). Interface hides this — `write_chunk(offset, data)` is our contract; local impl writes at offset, S3 impl buffers until 5MB or calls UploadPart.
- **Thumbnail generation** needs raw bytes. Local: read from disk. S3: download to worker temp dir, generate, upload, delete temp. Worker handles both behind the interface → no caller change.
- **Random access for video streaming** (Range requests): local streams easily; S3 supports Range natively. Both proxied through backend auth layer — interface exposes `open_range(object_key, start, end)`.
- **Atomic delete** post-expiry: local `os.unlink`; S3 `DeleteObject`. Both idempotent.

**Verdict:** interface is sufficient. No constraint forces a redesign later.

### D. Docker Compose — Mac Mini (ARM64) + Droplet (x86_64)

**Images verified multi-arch:** `postgres:16-alpine`, `redis:7-alpine`, `nginx:1.27-alpine`, `python:3.12-slim` — all publish `linux/amd64` + `linux/arm64` manifests.

**Gotchas addressed in compose from day 1:**
- Named volumes for `postgres_data`, `redis_data`, `media_data` (bind mounts on Mac via VirtioFS are slow, especially for Postgres)
- `platform:` not pinned → Docker picks native arch. Build our own `chat-backend`/`chat-worker` images via buildx in CI to produce both manifests.
- Ports: bind to `127.0.0.1:<port>` on dev to avoid conflicts + accidental LAN exposure; Droplet relies on firewall + Cloudflare
- Healthchecks on every service so `depends_on: condition: service_healthy` gates startup (Postgres readiness is the usual flake)
- `.env.example` committed; `.env` gitignored

**Verdict:** works on Mac Mini M-series and x86_64 Droplet with one codebase.

### E. E2E encryption — audit + recommendation

**Flagging this as the one real architectural risk in the CHAT-M2-final spec.** Full Signal Protocol from day 1 adds substantial complexity:
- Prekey bundle mgmt (upload, rotate, deplete-alert)
- Session establishment on first message + out-of-order message handling
- **Multi-device history sync** is the hard problem — Signal itself still struggles here. Your spec requires "message history sync across devices," which conflicts with naive E2E.
- Key backup/restore UX (lose phone = lose history, unless we build encrypted cloud backup with PIN — another subsystem)
- Push notification content becomes "New message" only
- Estimated +5–8 dev-days and a large ongoing maintenance surface

**Recommendation (keeps the architecture E2E-ready without shipping Signal in v1):**
1. Message schema has `body_ciphertext BYTEA` + `encryption_version SMALLINT` from day 1.
2. v1 `encryption_version = 1` = server-side pgcrypto at-rest only (body still plaintext to server, but at-rest encrypted; `pgcrypto` uses a key from env var).
3. v2 `encryption_version = 2` = Signal E2E (ciphertext opaque to server). Schema already supports it; flip a per-chat flag and add prekey endpoints.
4. Document the v2 migration path in `docs/MIGRATION_PATHS.md` alongside the infra swaps.

**Please confirm** you're OK with E2E deferred to v2 under this scheme. If you want Signal in v1, add ~7 days to the estimate and plan to solve history-sync-with-E2E explicitly (likely: single-device v1, multi-device v2).

### F. Cloudflare free-tier notes (operational, not code)

- WS works; idle timeout ~100s → our 20s client ping + 60s server idle close stays well under
- Enable "WebSockets" in CF dashboard; set the chat hostname to "DNS only" if we hit limits, otherwise proxied is fine
- Real client IP via `CF-Connecting-IP` — nginx `real_ip` config trusts CF ranges only
- Cert pinning: pin Cloudflare edge leaf cert SPKI (pin #1) + your origin cert SPKI (pin #2 backup); document 90d rotation procedure and ship a kill-switch env var `DISABLE_CERT_PINNING=true` for dev and emergency

---

## Revised repo layout (reflecting audits)

```
ChatA20/
  backend/
    app/
      main.py                    # FastAPI + WS mount + /metrics + /healthz + /readyz
      config.py                  # pydantic-settings, all env-driven
      auth.py                    # JWT RS256 verify, JWKS endpoint
      db.py                      # asyncpg pool via DATABASE_URL
      cache.py                   # redis.asyncio via REDIS_URL (pub/sub + cache + queue)
      logging_.py                # structlog JSON
      metrics.py                 # prometheus_client
      storage/
        base.py                  # MediaStore Protocol
        local.py                 # LocalDiskMediaStore
        s3.py                    # S3MediaStore (stub, tested via moto in CI)
        factory.py               # picks impl from MEDIA_BACKEND env
      routers/
        chats.py messages.py gallery.py attachments.py devices.py health.py auth_jwks.py
      ws/
        connection.py            # in-process registry (one process's WS fds)
        fanout.py                # Redis pub/sub: subscribe to user:{id} channel
        handlers.py presence.py
      services/
        datetris_client.py       # match check, user lookup, push-forward
        media_pipeline.py        # thumbnail, mime sniff, size validation
        expiry.py                # schedule + cancel jobs (Redis stream + Postgres fallback)
      migrations/                # alembic, uses DATABASE_URL
    worker/
      main.py                    # standalone process: expiry + thumbnails + push
      queues.py                  # XREADGROUP consumer w/ Postgres SKIP LOCKED fallback
    tests/
      test_http.py test_ws.py test_expiry.py test_media_local.py test_media_s3_moto.py test_pubsub.py
    Dockerfile
    pyproject.toml
    README.md
  frontend/
    lib/chat/
      chat_client.dart gallery/ widgets/ models/ crypto/ (v2 placeholder)
    pubspec.yaml
  infra/
    docker-compose.yml           # chat-backend, chat-worker, postgres, redis, nginx
    docker-compose.prod.yml      # overrides (no exposed ports, real certs, log drivers)
    nginx/chat.conf              # HTTP/2, WS upgrade, gzip, permessage-deflate
    cloudflare/NOTES.md
    backup/
      pg_backup.sh media_rsync.sh
      crontab.example
  docs/
    MIGRATION_PATHS.md           # <-- required by spec
    INTEGRATION_DATETRIS.md      # endpoints Datetris must expose + webhook contracts
    SECURITY_AUDIT.md
    MIGRATION_FROM_STREAM.md
    POSTGRES_TUNING.md
    OPERATIONS_RUNBOOK.md        # backup/restore, cert rotation, scale-out
  harness/
    run_local.sh seed.py two_client_sim.py
  .env.example
```

## Revised effort estimate

| Milestone | Scope | Est. |
|---|---|---|
| M2a Infra scaffold | docker-compose (pg+redis+nginx+app+worker), config, logging, metrics, health, JWKS | 2 d |
| M2b DB + migrations | alembic, schema, pgcrypto at-rest | 1 d |
| M3 WebSocket + Redis fanout | connection mgr, pub/sub, reconnect replay, typing, read | 4 d |
| M4a MediaStore abstraction | interface + local impl + S3 impl (tested with moto) | 2 d |
| M4b Media pipeline | upload (chunked), thumbnail worker, authed serving, gallery | 3 d |
| M5 Expiry engine | single-view + timer via worker queue (Redis Streams + PG fallback) | 2 d |
| M6 Datetris integration | JWT+JWKS client, match check, webhook receiver, push forwarder | 2 d |
| M7 Flutter chat module | client, widgets, expiring viewer, cert pinning wiring | 5 d |
| M8 Test harness | two-instance local sim + integration tests + CI | 2 d |
| M9 Docs | MIGRATION_PATHS, INTEGRATION_DATETRIS, SECURITY_AUDIT, runbook, migration from Stream | 2 d |
| **Total** | | **~25 dev-days** (~5 weeks solo) |

E2E Signal (if you insist on v1): **+7 d** and multi-device history sync becomes a design spike.

## Before I start scaffolding — please confirm

1. **13 defaults above** — OK, or paste overrides?
2. **E2E: defer Signal to v2 with the `encryption_version` schema hook?** (strong recommend)
3. **Backend language stays Python 3.12 + FastAPI** (you said matches Datetris — confirm Datetris is also on Python/FastAPI so the stack is genuinely shared)?
4. **Domain for chat:** subdomain like `chat.datetris.<tld>` vs path prefix on the main domain? Affects Cloudflare + nginx + cert pinning setup.
5. **Prod host:** DO Droplet (which size? 2GB is tight with PG+Redis+app+worker; 4GB recommended) — or something else?
6. **Push path final call:** route through Datetris `POST /internal/notifications/push` (no FCM/APNs creds in chat service)?
7. Any budget constraint on image registry (GHCR free works; confirm OK)?

On your "[CHAT-M2-final] reply + revised plan + audit results" — this is it. Say go and I scaffold M2a (compose + skeleton) first so you can `docker compose up` end-to-end before any real features land.

[CHAT-M2-go-FINAL]

Final hosting decision: Mac Mini for v1 + initial production.

- Build on Mac Mini Docker Compose (free dev)
- Deploy on Mac Mini Docker Compose (free prod)
- Internet via Cloudflare Tunnel (free, no port forwarding)
- TLS + DDoS via Cloudflare free tier
- Migration trigger: 1000 active users → reassess (likely move to AWS using credits)

Build infrastructure-agnostic (Docker Compose, env vars, S3-compatible API for media). Migration to AWS / DO / other = config + container ship, not rewrite.

Other answers same:
1. 13 defaults OK
2. E2E deferred to v2 with encryption_version schema hook
3. Python 3.12 + FastAPI
4. chat.datetris.date subdomain via Cloudflare Tunnel
6. Push routed through Datetris
7. GHCR free

GO. Scaffold M2a. Get docker compose up working on Mac Mini.

AUTO-APPROVE.
